delete_device_function.description: Device function for which the local object needs to be deleted. Mandatory if object_type = LOCAL. Should not be provided for object_type = SHARED.

detailed.description: True - Includes annotations in the returned json to indicate the type of object, scope, and device function. False - Returns only config objects without annotations.

effective.description: True - Returns effective configuration (i.e hierarchically merged configuration). False - Returns committed configuration at a given scope.
effective.name: effective

get_device_function.description: Filters configuration specific to given device-function type. If not provided and view_type = LOCAL, then defaults to all device-function's.

get_object_type.description: Retrieves LOCAL or SHARED or both(default) configuration objects.

patch_object_type.description: LOCAL - To update local objects. SHARED - To update shared objects. Default - Defaults to SHARED if not provided
patch_object_type.name: object-type

patch_scope_id.description: Scope at which local object needs to be updated. Mandatory if object-type = LOCAL. Should not be provided for object-type = SHARED.

post_device_function.description: Device function for which the local object needs to be created. Mandatory if object_type = LOCAL. Should not be provided for object_type = SHARED.

post_object_type.description: LOCAL - To create local objects. SHARED - To create shared objects. Default - Defaults to SHARED if not provided

post_scope_id.description: Scope at which local object needs to be created. Mandatory if object-type = LOCAL. Should not be provided for object-type = SHARED.

view_type.description: LIBRARY - Returns shared objects in library. For LIBRARY, all other parameters listed below, except 'detailed' , will be ignored. LOCAL - Returns configurations for a scope and one or more device function based on parameters below.

aaa-bw-contract.description:
AAA bandwidth contract assignments - Associates a named AAA
bandwidth contract with a traffic direction and an optional association
scope (per-user or per-apgroup) within a role or interface. At most one contract can
be assigned per direction.

bw-contract.description:
List of bandwidth contract assignments keyed by traffic
direction. Each entry assigns a named AAA bandwidth contract to either
upstream or downstream traffic, with an optional
association scope of per-user or per-apgroup.

x-enumDescriptions.PER_APGROUP: Assign bandwidth contract per-apgroup (default is per-role).
x-enumDescriptions.PER_USER: Assign bandwidth contract per-user (default is per-role).

x-enumDescriptions.DOWNSTREAM: Assign bandwidth contract to downstream traffic.
x-enumDescriptions.UPSTREAM: Assign bandwidth contract to upstream traffic.

app-aaa-contract.description:
Application-based AAA bandwidth contracts. Associates named AAA bandwidth
contracts with specific DPI applications and a traffic direction, allowing
per-application rate limiting.

app.description:
List of application-based bandwidth contracts keyed by application name
and traffic direction. Each entry maps a specific DPI application
(e.g. facebook, youtube, zoom) to a named AAA bandwidth contract for
either upstream or downstream traffic.

appname-direction.description: Key combination of application name and direction separated by '~'.

ArubaAaaBandwidthContract_AppCatBasedAaa.description: App category based AAA Bandwidth contracts.

app-category-aaa-contract.description:
Application category-based AAA bandwidth contracts. Associates named AAA
bandwidth contracts with DPI application categories and a traffic direction,
allowing rate limiting to be applied at the category level rather than
per individual application.

app-category.description:
List of application category-based bandwidth contracts keyed by
category name and traffic direction. Each entry maps a DPI application
category (e.g. STREAMING, GAMING, SOCIAL-NETWORKING) to a named AAA
bandwidth contract for either upstream or downstream traffic.

category-name-direction.description: Key combination of application category name and direction separated by '~'.

exclude-app-contract.description:
Exclude specific DPI applications from bandwidth contract enforcement.
Applications listed here will bypass bandwidth contract rate limiting
even if a matching contract is configured.

exclude-app.description:
List of DPI applications excluded from bandwidth contract enforcement.
Each entry specifies an application name that will bypass any configured
bandwidth contract rate limits.

exclude-app-cat-contract.description:

enforcement. Application categories listed here will bypass bandwidth

exclude-app-category.description:

bandwidth contract rate limits.

exclude-app-cat-contract.description:

ArubaAaaBandwidthContract_WebCatBasedAaa.description: WebCC category based AAA Bandwidth contracts.

web-category-aaa-contract.description:
WebCC category-based AAA bandwidth contracts. Associates named AAA bandwidth
contracts with WebCC content categories and a traffic direction, enabling
rate limiting based on the type of web content being accessed
(e.g. BUSINESS-ECONOMY, GAMES, EDUCATIONAL-INSTITUTIONS).

web-category.description:
List of WebCC category-based bandwidth contracts keyed by WebCC category
name and traffic direction. Each entry maps a WebCC content category to
a named AAA bandwidth contract for either upstream or downstream traffic.
There can be at most one contract per category per direction.

webcategory-name-direction.description: Key combination of WebCC category name and direction separated by '~'.

ArubaAaaBandwidthContract_WebRepBasedAaa.description: WebCC Reputation based AAA Bandwidth contracts.

web-reputation-aaa-contract.description:
WebCC reputation-based AAA bandwidth contracts. Associates named AAA bandwidth
contracts with WebCC reputation risk levels and a traffic direction, enabling
differentiated rate limiting based on the trustworthiness of web destinations.
Risk levels are: TRUSTWORTHY (81-100), LOW_RISK (61-80), MODERATE_RISK (41-60),
SUSPICIOUS (21-40), and HIGH_RISK (1-20).

web-reputation.description:
List of WebCC reputation-based bandwidth contracts keyed by reputation
risk level and traffic direction. Each entry maps a WebCC reputation
level (TRUSTWORTHY, LOW_RISK, MODERATE_RISK, SUSPICIOUS, or HIGH_RISK)
to a named AAA bandwidth contract for either upstream or downstream
traffic. There can be at most one contract per risk level per direction.

webrepname-direction.description: Key combination of WebCC reputation name and direction separated by '~'.

ArubaPolicyCondition_AggregateRulesInfo.description:
Metadata for policy rule aggregation by role. Groups multiple role-based rules together
for optimized evaluation and reporting. Used internally to track related rules.

aggregate-info.description:
Read-only aggregation metadata for role-based policy rules. Only applicable when the address
type is ADDRESS_ROLE. Contains information about related rules that have been grouped together
for efficient evaluation.

agg-roles.description:
List of roles that have been aggregated together in the policy evaluation. Each entry
includes the role name and its corresponding rule position for tracking purposes.

agg-role.description: Name of the user role that has been aggregated into this policy rule group. Maximum 256 characters.

agg-rule-pos.description:
Position of the rule within the aggregated group. Used to track the evaluation order
of rules within the same aggregation set.

ArubaPolicyCondition_AggregateRulesInfoG.description:
Metadata for policy rule aggregation by role. Groups multiple role-based rules together
for optimized evaluation and reporting. Used internally to track related rules.

aggregate-info.description:
Read-only aggregation metadata for role-based policy rules. Only applicable when the address
type is ADDRESS_ROLE. Contains information about related rules that have been grouped together
for efficient evaluation.

agg-roles.description:
List of roles that have been aggregated together in the policy evaluation. Each entry
includes the role name and its corresponding rule position for tracking purposes.

agg-role.description: Name of the user role that has been aggregated into this policy rule group. Maximum 256 characters.

agg-rule-pos.description:
Position of the rule within the aggregated group. Used to track the evaluation order
of rules within the same aggregation set.

ArubaPolicyCondition_DestinationConfig.description:
Complete destination address matching configuration for policy rules. Defines all destination
address criteria including IP addresses, roles, groups, domain names, and aliases with optional
role-based refinements.

destination.description:
Destination address matching criteria for the policy rule. Specifies which destination IP
addresses, roles, domain names, or address groups the rule applies to. The match type is
determined by the type field.

address-group.description:
Reference to a pre-configured IPv4 or IPv6 address group object. Only applicable when type is
ADDRESS_GROUP. Address groups allow you to create reusable sets of destination IP addresses or subnets.
The address group must be created before it can be referenced in a policy.

domain-name.description:
Destination domain name (FQDN) for matching. Only applicable when type is ADDRESS_DOMAIN_NAME.
Matches traffic destined to the specified fully qualified domain name. Uses DNS resolution to
match against the domain's IP addresses. Maximum 256 characters. For AP devices only.

host-address.description:
Destination host IP address matching. Only applicable when type is ADDRESS_HOST. Specify either
an IPv4 address, IPv6 address, or reference to a host alias. Only one addressing method can be
used at a time.

invert.description:
Inverts the destination match logic. When set to true, matches all destinations EXCEPT the
specified destination. For AP devices only. Defaults to false.

net-group.description:
Reference to a named network group (net-group) for destination address matching. Only applicable
when type is ADDRESS_ALIAS. Network groups allow you to create reusable sets of destination IP
addresses or subnets. The network group must be created before it can be referenced.

network-address.description:
Destination network IP prefix matching using CIDR notation. Only applicable when type is
ADDRESS_NETWORK. Specify either an IPv4 prefix (e.g., 10.0.0.0/8), IPv6 prefix (e.g., 2001:db8::/32),
or reference to a network alias. Only one addressing method can be used at a time.

role.description:
Name of the user role for destination address matching. Only applicable when type is ADDRESS_ROLE.
Matches traffic destined to users or resources assigned to this role. Maximum 256 characters.
For switch and gateway devices only.

role-list.description:
List of user role names for destination address matching. Only applicable when type is ADDRESS_ROLE.
Matches traffic destined to users or resources assigned to any of the specified roles. Each role
name can be up to 256 characters. For switch and gateway devices only.

items.description:
List of user role names for destination address matching. Only applicable when type is ADDRESS_ROLE.
Matches traffic destined to users or resources assigned to any of the specified roles. Each role
name can be up to 256 characters. For switch and gateway devices only.

subnet-address.description:
Destination network address with subnet mask notation. Only applicable when type is ADDRESS_SUBNET_MASK.
Specify an IPv4 or IPv6 network with a subnet mask (e.g., 192.168.1.0/255.255.255.0). For switch
devices only. The network-subnet-address field is required.

network-subnet-address.description: An IPv4 or IPv6 prefix followed by a slash and a subnet mask.

oneOf.x-typeDescription: An IPv4 prefix represented in dotted quad notation followed by a slash and a subnet mask in dotted quad notation.
An IPv6 prefix represented in full, shortened, or mixed shortened format followed by a slash and another IPv6-formatted subnet mask. Must be a contiguous network mask.

type.description:
Type of destination address matching to perform. Determines which destination address fields are
applicable: ADDRESS_ANY matches all destinations, ADDRESS_HOST matches a single IP, ADDRESS_NETWORK
matches a subnet, ADDRESS_ALIAS matches a named address group, ADDRESS_ROLE matches traffic to
specific user roles, ADDRESS_DOMAIN_NAME matches by FQDN, ADDRESS_GROUP matches an object-group,
and various other device-specific options.

x-enumDescriptions.ADDRESS_SUBNET_MASK: IPv4 or IPv6 address with subnet mask.

Specify an IPv4 or IPv6 network with a subnet mask (e.g., 192.168.1.0/255.255.255.0). For switch
devices only. The network-subnet-address field is required.

network-subnet-address.description: An IPv4 or IPv6 prefix followed by a slash and a subnet mask.

oneOf.x-typeDescription: An IPv4 prefix represented in dotted quad notation followed by a slash and a subnet mask in dotted quad notation.
An IPv6 prefix represented in full, shortened, or mixed shortened format followed by a slash and another IPv6-formatted subnet mask. Must be a contiguous network mask.

type.description:
Type of destination address matching to perform. Determines which destination address fields are
applicable: ADDRESS_ANY matches all destinations, ADDRESS_HOST matches a single IP, ADDRESS_NETWORK
matches a subnet, ADDRESS_ALIAS matches a named address group, ADDRESS_ROLE matches traffic to
specific user roles, ADDRESS_DOMAIN_NAME matches by FQDN, ADDRESS_GROUP matches an object-group,
and various other device-specific options.

x-enumDescriptions.ADDRESS_SUBNET_MASK: IPv4 or IPv6 address with subnet mask.

ArubaPolicyCondition_DestinationRoleOpti.description:
Additional matching options for destination role-based policies. Allows combining role-based
matching with specific IP addresses or network prefixes to create more granular policies.

host-address.description:
Destination host IP address for role option refinement. Only applicable when options-type is
ADDRESS_HOST. Specify either an IPv4 address, IPv6 address, or reference to a host alias to
further restrict role-based destination matching. Only one addressing method can be used at a time.

host-address-alias.description:
Reference to a pre-configured host address alias. The alias must be of type ALIAS_HOST.
Use this to reference a named host IP address instead of specifying the IP directly.

net-group.description:
Reference to a named network group for destination role option refinement. Only applicable when
options-type is ADDRESS_ALIAS. Use this to further restrict role-based destination matching to
a specific network group.

network-address.description:
Destination network IP prefix for role option refinement. Only applicable when options-type is
ADDRESS_NETWORK. Specify either an IPv4 prefix, IPv6 prefix, or reference to a network alias to
further restrict role-based destination matching. Only one addressing method can be used at a time.

ip-prefix-alias.description:
Reference to a pre-configured network address alias. The alias must be of type ALIAS_NETWORK.
Use this to reference a named IP prefix instead of specifying the network directly.

options-type.description:
Type of additional destination matching to apply in conjunction with role-based matching.
Only applicable when the address type is ADDRESS_ROLE. Allows filtering role traffic to
specific hosts, networks, or address aliases.

options-type.description:

ArubaPolicyCondition_EthernetHeaderConfi.description:
Defines Layer 2 Ethernet frame matching criteria including MAC addresses, EtherType,
VLAN tags, and priority values. Used for MAC ACL rules and advanced Layer 2 filtering.

ethernet-header.description:
Layer 2 Ethernet frame matching criteria. Specifies MAC addresses, EtherType, VLAN identifiers,
and priority code points to match against. Used for MAC-based access control and Layer 2 filtering.

destination-mac.description:
Destination MAC address to match in Ethernet frames. Specified in IEEE 802 format (e.g.,
aa:bb:cc:dd:ee:ff). Use with destination-mac-mask for wildcard matching of MAC address ranges.

destination-mac-mask.description:
Wildcard mask for destination MAC address matching. Specified in IEEE 802 format. A zero bit
indicates the corresponding bit in the MAC address must match; a one bit indicates don't care.
Use this for matching ranges of MAC addresses.

ethermask.description:
Wildcard mask for EtherType matching. A zero bit indicates the corresponding bit in the
EtherType must match; a one bit indicates don't care. Use this for matching ranges of
EtherType values. For gateway devices only.

ethertype.description:
EtherType field value to match in Ethernet frames. Identifies the protocol encapsulated in
the payload (e.g., 0x0800 for IPv4, 0x86DD for IPv6, 0x0806 for ARP). Use with ethermask
for wildcard matching.

x-enumDescriptions.ETHERTYPE_VLAN:
VLAN-tagged frame (as defined by IEEE 802.1q) (0x8100). Note
that this value is also used to represent Shortest Path
Bridging (IEEE 801.1aq) frames.

oneOf.x-typeDescription: Ethernet Protocol types.

pcp.description:
IEEE 802.1Q Priority Code Point (PCP) value to match in tagged Ethernet frames. Specifies
the priority level for QoS (0=lowest, 7=highest). Only applicable for VLAN-tagged traffic.
For switch devices only. Valid range: 0-7.

source-mac.description:
Source MAC address to match in Ethernet frames. Specified in IEEE 802 format (e.g., aa:bb:cc:dd:ee:ff).
Use with source-mac-mask for wildcard matching of MAC address ranges.

source-mac-mask.description:
Wildcard mask for source MAC address matching. Specified in IEEE 802 format. A zero bit indicates
the corresponding bit in the MAC address must match; a one bit indicates don't care. Use this
for matching ranges of MAC addresses.

vlan.description:
VLAN identifier to match in IEEE 802.1Q tagged Ethernet frames. Use this to match traffic
on a specific VLAN. For switch devices only. Valid range: 0-4000.

ArubaPolicyCondition_IpProtocolFieldsCom.description:
Defines IP header matching criteria common to both IPv4 and IPv6. Includes protocol type,
DSCP, ToS, ECN, TTL/hop-limit, and protocol-specific fields for ICMP and IGMP.

ip-header.description:
IP header matching criteria. Specifies Layer 3 packet header fields to match including protocol,
quality of service markings (DSCP, ToS), hop limit (TTL), and protocol-specific parameters for
ICMP and IGMP.

dscp.description:
Differentiated Services Code Point (DSCP) value to match in the IP header. Used for identifying
and classifying traffic based on QoS markings. Valid values are DSCP keywords (e.g., default, af11,
ef) or numeric values (0-63). Cannot be used simultaneously with IP Precedence or ToS fields.

x-enumDescriptions.AF11: DSCP 10 (Assured Forwarding class 1, low drop probability)
x-enumDescriptions.AF12: DSCP 12 (Assured Forwarding class 1, medium drop probability)
x-enumDescriptions.AF13: DSCP 14 (Assured Forwarding class 1, high drop probability)
x-enumDescriptions.AF21: DSCP 18 (Assured Forwarding class 2, low drop probability)
x-enumDescriptions.AF22: DSCP 20 (Assured Forwarding class 2, medium drop probability)
x-enumDescriptions.AF23: DSCP 22 (Assured Forwarding class 2, high drop probability)
x-enumDescriptions.AF31: DSCP 26 (Assured Forwarding class 3, low drop probability)
x-enumDescriptions.AF32: DSCP 28 (Assured Forwarding class 3, medium drop probability)
x-enumDescriptions.AF33: DSCP 30 (Assured Forwarding class 3, high drop probability)
x-enumDescriptions.AF41: DSCP 34 (Assured Forwarding class 4, low drop probability)
x-enumDescriptions.AF42: DSCP 36 (Assured Forwarding class 4, medium drop probability)
x-enumDescriptions.AF43: DSCP 38 (Assured Forwarding class 4, high drop probability)

oneOf.x-typeDescription: DSCP value enum types

ecn.description:
Explicit Congestion Notification (ECN) bits to match in the IP header. Used to signal network
congestion without dropping packets. Cannot be used simultaneously with IP Precedence or ToS fields.
ecn.x-typeDescription: Explicit Congestion Notification (ECN) marking in the IP header.

fragment.description:
Match IP packets with the fragmentation flag set or that are fragmented. When set to true,
matches fragmented packets only. For CX switches only. Used to identify and handle packet
fragmentation scenarios.

hop-limit.description:
Hop limit to match in IP packets. Known as TTL (Time To Live) in IPv4 packets and hop limit in
IPv6 packets. Specifies the maximum number of hops (router traversals) a packet can make before
being discarded. Valid range: 0-255.

icmp.description:
ICMP (Internet Control Message Protocol) matching criteria. Only applicable when the protocol
is ICMP (1) or ICMPv6 (58). Specifies ICMP message type and code to match against (e.g., echo
request, destination unreachable).

icmp-code.description:
ICMP message code as a numeric value. Provides additional specificity for the ICMP type
(e.g., for type 3 destination-unreachable: code 0=network-unreachable, code 1=host-unreachable).
Used in conjunction with icmp-type. Valid range: 0-255.

icmp-enum.description:
ICMP message type using predefined enumeration values (e.g., echo-request, echo-reply,
destination-unreachable). Provides a user-friendly way to specify common ICMP types.

icmp-type.description:
ICMP message type as a numeric value (e.g., 0=echo-reply, 3=destination-unreachable, 8=echo-request).
Use with icmp-code to match specific ICMP messages. Valid range: 0-255.

igmp.description:
IGMP (Internet Group Management Protocol) matching criteria. Only applicable when the protocol
is IGMP. Specifies IGMP message type to match against (e.g., membership query, membership report).

type.description:
IGMP message type using predefined enumeration values (e.g., membership-query, membership-report,
leave-group). Used to match specific IGMP protocol messages for multicast group management.

ip-precedence.description:
IP Precedence value to match in the IP ToS field. Legacy QoS field used for traffic priority.
Valid range: 0-7 (0=routine, 7=network control). Cannot be used simultaneously with DSCP or ECN
fields. For switch devices only.

ipv6-fragmentation-header.description:
IPv6 fragmentation header value to match. Used to identify fragmented IPv6 packets. Only
applicable for IPv6 traffic.

protocol.description:
IP protocol to match in the packet. Can be specified either as a protocol number (e.g., 6 for TCP,
17 for UDP, 1 for ICMP) or by a named protocol identifier (e.g., IP_TCP, IP_UDP, IP_ICMP). This
determines which transport protocol the rule applies to.

oneOf.x-typeDescription: IP Protocol types.

tos.description:
Type of Service (ToS) value to match in the IP header. Legacy QoS field used for traffic
classification. Valid range: 0-63. Cannot be used simultaneously with DSCP or ECN fields.
For switch devices only.

vlan.description:
VLAN identifier to match in IP packets. Matches traffic on the specified VLAN. Valid range:
1-4094. For switch devices only. Use this to apply policies to traffic on specific VLANs.

ArubaPolicyCondition_PortConfig.description:
Port matching criteria with comparison operator. Specifies how to match against port numbers:
single port (equals), port range (minimum and maximum), or port comparison (less than,
greater than). When operator is COMPARISON_RANGE, both min and max values are required;
otherwise only the min value is used.

group.description:
Reference to a pre-configured port group object. Only applicable when operator is PORT_GROUP.
The port group allows you to match against multiple ports or port ranges defined in a single
reusable object. The port group must be created before it can be referenced in a policy.

max.description:
Upper bound of port range. Only applicable when operator is COMPARISON_RANGE. Must be
greater than or equal to the min value. Valid range: 0-65535.
max.x-typeDescription:
A 16-bit port number used by a transport protocol such as TCP
or UDP.

min.description:
Port number to match, or the lower bound of a port range when operator is COMPARISON_RANGE.
For single port matching, this is the exact port number. Valid range: 0-65535.
min.x-typeDescription:
A 16-bit port number used by a transport protocol such as TCP
or UDP.

operator.description:
Comparison operator for port matching. Specifies how to compare the packet's port number
against the configured value: equals, not equals, less than, greater than, range, or
reference to a port group object.

x-enumDescriptions.COMPARISON_RANGE: Minimum and maximum values of range.

ArubaPolicyCondition_ServiceConfig.description:
Defines application-aware and network service matching criteria for policy rules,
including applications, application categories, web categories, web reputation,
and named network services.

services.description:
Application-aware and network service matching criteria. Specifies which
applications, web categories, or network services the policy rule applies to.

app-category.description:
Application category to match (e.g., collaboration, file-sharing, messaging). Only
applicable when the rule type is RULE_APP_CATEGORY. Matches all applications belonging
to the specified category.

application.description:
Specific application to match (e.g., Zoom, Dropbox, Netflix). Only applicable when
the rule type is RULE_APPLICATION. Uses deep packet inspection (DPI) to identify
the application regardless of port or protocol.

apptag-name.description:
Aruba application tag name for application visibility and control. Only applicable when
the rule type is RULE_APPTAG. Must be used in conjunction with apptag-type. Identifies
an application discovered through Aruba's application classification.

apptag-type.description:
Aruba application tag type classification. Only applicable when the rule type is RULE_APPTAG.
Must be used in conjunction with apptag-name. Categorizes the application for visibility
and policy enforcement.

net-service.description:
Named network service to match. Only applicable when the rule type is RULE_NET_SERVICE.
References a pre-configured network service that defines protocol, ports, and other
transport-layer parameters.

web-category.description:
Web content category to match (e.g., social-media, gaming, streaming). Only applicable
when the rule type is RULE_WEB_CATEGORY or RULE_WEB_CAT_REP. Used for URL filtering.

web-reputation.description:
Web reputation score to match (e.g., trustworthy, suspicious, malicious). Only applicable
when the rule type is RULE_WEB_REPUTATION or RULE_WEB_CAT_REP. Used for threat prevention.

x-enumDescriptions.HIGH_RISK: Reputation score of 1-20 is deemed high-risk.
x-enumDescriptions.LOW_RISK: Reputation score of 61-80 is deemed low-risk.
x-enumDescriptions.MODERATE_RISK: Reputation score of 41-60 is deemed moderate-risk.
x-enumDescriptions.SUSPICIOUS: Reputation score of 21-40 is deemed suspicious.
x-enumDescriptions.TRUSTWORTHY: Reputation score of 81-100 is deemed Trustworthy.

web-reputation.x-typeDescription: WebCC URL reputation score ranges that determine risk level.

ArubaPolicyCondition_SourceBaseConfig.description:
Defines source address matching criteria for policy rules. Supports various address types
including individual hosts, networks, address groups, roles, and aliases.

address-group.description:
Reference to a pre-configured IPv4 or IPv6 address group object. Only applicable when type
is ADDRESS_GROUP. Address groups allow you to create reusable sets of IP addresses or subnets.
The address group must be created before it can be referenced in a policy.

host-address.description:
Source host IP address matching. Only applicable when type is ADDRESS_HOST. Specify either
an IPv4 address, IPv6 address, or reference to a host alias. Only one addressing method
can be used at a time.

network-address.description:
Source network IP prefix matching using CIDR notation. Only applicable when type is ADDRESS_NETWORK.
Specify either an IPv4 prefix (e.g., 10.0.0.0/8), IPv6 prefix (e.g., 2001:db8::/32), or reference
to a network alias. Only one addressing method can be used at a time.

subnet-address.description:
Source network address with subnet mask notation. Only applicable when type is ADDRESS_SUBNET_MASK.
Specify an IPv4 or IPv6 network with a subnet mask (e.g., 192.168.1.0/255.255.255.0). For
switch devices only. The network-subnet-address field is required.

network-subnet-address.description:
IPv4 or IPv6 network address followed by a slash and subnet mask (e.g., 192.168.1.0/255.255.255.0
or 2001:db8::/ffff:ffff::). This is required when using ADDRESS_SUBNET_MASK type.

oneOf.x-typeDescription: An IPv4 prefix represented in dotted quad notation followed by a slash and a subnet mask in dotted quad notation.
An IPv6 prefix represented in full, shortened, or mixed shortened format followed by a slash and another IPv6-formatted subnet mask. Must be a contiguous network mask.

type.description:
Type of source address matching to perform. Determines which source address fields are applicable:
ADDRESS_ANY matches all sources, ADDRESS_HOST matches a single IP, ADDRESS_NETWORK matches a
subnet, ADDRESS_ALIAS matches a named address group, ADDRESS_ROLE matches traffic from specific
user roles, ADDRESS_GROUP matches an object-group, and various other device-specific options.

x-enumDescriptions.ADDRESS_SUBNET_MASK: IPv4 or IPv6 address with subnet mask.

ArubaPolicyCondition_SourceBaseConfigGet.description:
Defines source address matching criteria for policy rules. Supports various address types
including individual hosts, networks, address groups, roles, and aliases.

address-group.description:
Reference to a pre-configured IPv4 or IPv6 address group object. Only applicable when type
is ADDRESS_GROUP. Address groups allow you to create reusable sets of IP addresses or subnets.
The address group must be created before it can be referenced in a policy.

host-address.description:
Source host IP address matching. Only applicable when type is ADDRESS_HOST. Specify either
an IPv4 address, IPv6 address, or reference to a host alias. Only one addressing method
can be used at a time.

network-address.description:
Source network IP prefix matching using CIDR notation. Only applicable when type is ADDRESS_NETWORK.
Specify either an IPv4 prefix (e.g., 10.0.0.0/8), IPv6 prefix (e.g., 2001:db8::/32), or reference
to a network alias. Only one addressing method can be used at a time.

subnet-address.description:
Source network address with subnet mask notation. Only applicable when type is ADDRESS_SUBNET_MASK.
Specify an IPv4 or IPv6 network with a subnet mask (e.g., 192.168.1.0/255.255.255.0). For
switch devices only. The network-subnet-address field is required.

network-subnet-address.description:
IPv4 or IPv6 network address followed by a slash and subnet mask (e.g., 192.168.1.0/255.255.255.0
or 2001:db8::/ffff:ffff::). This is required when using ADDRESS_SUBNET_MASK type.

oneOf.x-typeDescription: An IPv4 prefix represented in dotted quad notation followed by a slash and a subnet mask in dotted quad notation.
An IPv6 prefix represented in full, shortened, or mixed shortened format followed by a slash and another IPv6-formatted subnet mask. Must be a contiguous network mask.

type.description:
Type of source address matching to perform. Determines which source address fields are applicable:
ADDRESS_ANY matches all sources, ADDRESS_HOST matches a single IP, ADDRESS_NETWORK matches a
subnet, ADDRESS_ALIAS matches a named address group, ADDRESS_ROLE matches traffic from specific
user roles, ADDRESS_GROUP matches an object-group, and various other device-specific options.

x-enumDescriptions.ADDRESS_SUBNET_MASK: IPv4 or IPv6 address with subnet mask.

ArubaPolicyCondition_SourceConfig.description:
Complete source address matching configuration for policy rules. Defines all source address
criteria including IP addresses, roles, groups, and aliases with optional role-based refinements.

source.description:
Source address matching criteria for the policy rule. Specifies which source IP addresses,
roles, or address groups the rule applies to. The match type is determined by the type field.

net-group.description:
Reference to a named network group (net-group) for source address matching. Only applicable
when type is ADDRESS_ALIAS. Network groups allow you to create reusable sets of IP addresses
or subnets. The network group must be created before it can be referenced.

role.description:
Name of the user role for source address matching. Only applicable when type is ADDRESS_ROLE.
Matches traffic originating from users assigned to this role. Maximum 256 characters. For
switch and gateway devices only.

role-list.description:
List of user role names for source address matching. Only applicable when type is ADDRESS_ROLE.
Matches traffic originating from users assigned to any of the specified roles. Each role name
can be up to 256 characters. For switch and gateway devices only.

items.description:
List of user role names for source address matching. Only applicable when type is ADDRESS_ROLE.
Matches traffic originating from users assigned to any of the specified roles. Each role name
can be up to 256 characters. For switch and gateway devices only.

ArubaPolicyCondition_SourceConfigGet.description:
Complete source address matching configuration for policy rules. Defines all source address
criteria including IP addresses, roles, groups, and aliases with optional role-based refinements.

source.description:

roles, or address groups the rule applies to. The match type is determined by the type field.

net-group.description:

role.description:

switch and gateway devices only.

role-list.description:
List of user role names for source address matching. Only applicable when type is ADDRESS_ROLE.
Matches traffic originating from users assigned to any of the specified roles. Each role name
can be up to 256 characters. For switch and gateway devices only.

items.description:
List of user role names for source address matching. Only applicable when type is ADDRESS_ROLE.
Matches traffic originating from users assigned to any of the specified roles. Each role name

ArubaPolicyCondition_SourceRoleOptions.description:
Additional matching options for source role-based policies. Allows combining role-based
matching with specific IP addresses or network prefixes to create more granular policies.

host-address.description:
Source host IP address for role option refinement. Only applicable when options-type is ADDRESS_HOST.
Specify either an IPv4 address, IPv6 address, or reference to a host alias to further restrict
role-based source matching. Only one addressing method can be used at a time.

host-address-alias.description:
Reference to a pre-configured host address alias. The alias must be of type ALIAS_HOST.
Use this to reference a named host IP address instead of specifying the IP directly.

net-group.description:
Reference to a named network group for source role option refinement. Only applicable when
options-type is ADDRESS_ALIAS. Use this to further restrict role-based source matching to
a specific network group.

network-address.description:
Source network IP prefix for role option refinement. Only applicable when options-type is
ADDRESS_NETWORK. Specify either an IPv4 prefix, IPv6 prefix, or reference to a network alias
to further restrict role-based source matching. Only one addressing method can be used at a time.

ip-prefix-alias.description:
Reference to a pre-configured network address alias. The alias must be of type ALIAS_NETWORK.
Use this to reference a named IP prefix instead of specifying the network directly.

options-type.description:
Type of additional source matching to apply in conjunction with role-based matching. Only
applicable when the address type is ADDRESS_ROLE. Allows filtering role traffic from specific
hosts, networks, or address aliases.

ArubaPolicyCondition_SourceRoleOptionsGe.description:

ArubaPolicyCondition_TransportFieldsConf.description:
Defines transport-layer (Layer 4) matching criteria including source and destination ports,
TCP flags, and connection state. Applicable for TCP, UDP, and SCTP protocols.

transport-fields.description:
Transport-layer (Layer 4) packet matching criteria. Specifies source and destination ports,
TCP flags, and connection state to match against. Only applicable for TCP, UDP, and SCTP protocols.

destination-port.description:
Destination port number or range to match. Only applicable when the protocol is TCP (6),
UDP (17), or SCTP (132). Use this to match traffic destined to specific services or applications.

destination-port-net-group.description:
Reference to a named network service for destination port matching. The network service defines
a reusable set of ports and protocols for matching traffic destined to specific services.

established.description:
Match packets belonging to an established TCP connection. Only applicable for TCP traffic.
When set to true, matches TCP packets with ACK or RST flags set, indicating the connection
is already established. Useful for stateful firewall rules to allow return traffic.
Cannot be used simultaneously with both TCP_RST and TCP_ACK flags. Defaults to false.

source-port.description:
Source port number or range to match. Only applicable when the protocol is TCP (6), UDP (17),
or SCTP (132). Use this to match traffic originating from specific ports.

source-port-net-group.description:
Reference to a named network service for source port matching. The network service defines
a reusable set of ports and protocols for matching traffic originating from specific services.

tcp-flags.description:
List of TCP control flags to match (e.g., SYN, ACK, FIN, RST). Only applicable for TCP traffic.
Use this to match specific TCP connection states or control packets. Multiple flags can be specified.

items.description:
List of TCP control flags to match (e.g., SYN, ACK, FIN, RST). Only applicable for TCP traffic.
Use this to match specific TCP connection states or control packets. Multiple flags can be specified.

x-enumDescriptions.TCP_ECE:
TCP ECN-Echo flag.  If the SYN flag is set, indicates that
the TCP peer is ECN-capable, otherwise indicates that a
packet with Congestion Experienced flag in the IP header
is set

ArubaPolicyGroup_PolicyGrouping.description: Defines the ordered list of firewall policies and their global evaluation sequence.

policy-group-list.description:
An ordered list of firewall policies with their evaluation priorities.
Policies are evaluated in ascending order by position (lower values first).
Each policy name must be unique, and each position must be unique within the group.

description.description:
A user-defined description explaining the purpose or context of this policy's
placement in the evaluation sequence. Maximum length is 256 characters.

name.description:
Unique identifier for the firewall policy. This name must reference an existing
policy configured in the system. Maximum length is 256 characters.

position.description:
The evaluation priority of this policy in the global policy sequence. Policies are
evaluated in ascending order (lower numbers are evaluated first). This value must be
unique across all policies in the group. Valid range: 1 to 2000.

position.description:

Global policy group configuration that defines the evaluation sequence for all
firewall policies across the network. Use this to control the order in which

policy-group.description: Configuration for the ordered list of firewall policies and their evaluation priorities.

ArubaPolicy_ActionConfig.description:
Grouping for the action associated with a policy rule. Defines
the forwarding action (permit, deny, redirect, NAT, route, etc.)
and optional secondary actions (QoS, logging, mirroring, etc.).

action.description:
Specifies the action to take when traffic matches the rule
condition. Exactly one primary forwarding action type must be
configured. Secondary actions (logging, QoS, mirroring) can
be combined with the primary action.

secondary-actions.description:
Container for secondary actions that can be applied alongside
the primary forwarding action. Includes QoS settings (DSCP,
dot1p, rate-limit), logging, mirroring, denylist, bandwidth
throttling, and packet counting.

append-mirror-session.description:
Additional mirror session numbers to copy matched traffic
to. Up to 3 sessions can be appended. For PVOS switches only.

items.description:
Additional mirror session numbers to copy matched traffic
to. Up to 3 sessions can be appended. For PVOS switches only.
items.x-typeDescription: Type definition representing a Mirror session

cos.description:
Sets the Layer 2 802.1Q Class of Service (CoS) value
(0-7) on matched packets. For CX switches only.

count.description:
When set to true, enables counting of packets matching
this rule for monitoring purposes. Default: false.

denylist.description:
When set to true, adds the source of matching traffic
to the deny list (formerly blacklist). Default: false.

disable-scanning.description:
When set to true, disables application scanning for
matched traffic on AP devices. Default: false.

dot1p-priority.description:
Sets the Layer 2 802.1Q Priority Code Point (PCP) value
(0-7) on matched packets for QoS prioritization.

dscp.description:
Sets the Differentiated Services Code Point (DSCP) value
on matched packets for QoS classification. Not applicable
when the action type is ACTION_PACKET_MARKING.

x-enumDescriptions.AF11: DSCP 10 (Assured Forwarding class 1, low drop probability)
x-enumDescriptions.AF12: DSCP 12 (Assured Forwarding class 1, medium drop probability)
x-enumDescriptions.AF13: DSCP 14 (Assured Forwarding class 1, high drop probability)
x-enumDescriptions.AF21: DSCP 18 (Assured Forwarding class 2, low drop probability)
x-enumDescriptions.AF22: DSCP 20 (Assured Forwarding class 2, medium drop probability)
x-enumDescriptions.AF23: DSCP 22 (Assured Forwarding class 2, high drop probability)
x-enumDescriptions.AF31: DSCP 26 (Assured Forwarding class 3, low drop probability)
x-enumDescriptions.AF32: DSCP 28 (Assured Forwarding class 3, medium drop probability)
x-enumDescriptions.AF33: DSCP 30 (Assured Forwarding class 3, high drop probability)
x-enumDescriptions.AF41: DSCP 34 (Assured Forwarding class 4, low drop probability)
x-enumDescriptions.AF42: DSCP 36 (Assured Forwarding class 4, medium drop probability)
x-enumDescriptions.AF43: DSCP 38 (Assured Forwarding class 4, high drop probability)

oneOf.x-typeDescription: DSCP value enum types

ip-precedence.description:
Sets the IP precedence value (0-7) on matched packets.
Not applicable when the action type is ACTION_PACKET_MARKING.

local-queue-priority.description:
Sets the local queue priority for matched traffic.
Can be a numeric value (0-7) or a named queue type.

oneOf.x-typeDescription: Queue type.

log.description:
When set to true, enables logging of traffic matching
this rule. Default: false.

markapp.description:
Tags matched traffic with a custom application identifier
for prioritization. For AP devices only.

x-enumDescriptions.CUSTOM1: Selects the first custom application profile.
x-enumDescriptions.CUSTOM2: Selects the second custom application profile.
x-enumDescriptions.CUSTOM3: Selects the third custom application profile.
x-enumDescriptions.CUSTOM4: Selects the fourth custom application profile.
x-enumDescriptions.CUSTOM5: Selects the fifth custom application profile.

markapp.x-typeDescription:
Enumeration of custom application profiles that can be
assigned to policy rules for application-level marking
on access point devices.

mirror.description:
When set to true, mirrors matched traffic for analysis
or troubleshooting. For AP and GW devices. Default: false.

mirror-session.description:
Specifies the mirror session number to copy matched traffic
to for switch devices (CX/PVOS).
mirror-session.x-typeDescription: Type definition representing a Mirror session

rate-limit.description:
Rate limit for matched traffic in kilobytes per second.
Traffic exceeding this rate is dropped. Valid range:
1 to 4294967295 kb/sec.

throttle-downstream.description:
Maximum downstream bandwidth in kilobytes per second for
matched traffic. Applies throttling from network to client.
For AP devices only. Valid range: 1 to 4294966 kbytes/sec.

throttle-upstream.description:
Maximum upstream bandwidth in kilobytes per second for
matched traffic. Applies throttling from client to network.
For AP devices only. Valid range: 1 to 4294966 kbytes/sec.

vxlan-encap.description:
When set to true, enables VXLAN encapsulation for matched
traffic. Default: false.

send-deny-response.description:
When set to true, sends a TCP RST or ICMP unreachable response
to the client when traffic is denied, instead of silently
dropping the packet. Only applicable when action type is
ACTION_DENY. Default: false.

type.description:
Specifies the primary forwarding action for this rule.
ACTION_ALLOW permits the traffic. ACTION_DENY drops the
traffic. ACTION_REDIRECT redirects to a configured destination.
ACTION_SOURCE_NAT/ACTION_DESTINATION_NAT/ACTION_DUAL_NAT apply
NAT translations. ACTION_ROUTE uses policy-based routing.
ACTION_QOS applies QoS marking. Exactly one action type must
be specified per rule.

x-enumDescriptions.ACTION_FORWARD_IPSEC_VPNC: Forward to IPSEC map to VPNC.

ArubaPolicy_ConditionConfig.description:
Grouping for the triggering condition of a policy rule. Defines
the match criteria including rule type, address family, source,
destination, protocol, transport fields, and service parameters.

condition.description:
Specifies the match condition for a policy rule. The condition
determines which traffic the rule applies to based on criteria
such as protocol, source/destination addresses, ports, and
application or service identifiers.

address-family.description:
IP address family for the policy rule. Specifies whether
the rule applies to IPv4 or IPv6 traffic.

rule-type.description:
Specifies the rule type that determines what kind of traffic
matching is performed. RULE_ANY matches all traffic.
RULE_TCP/RULE_UDP match TCP/UDP traffic respectively.
RULE_ACL_IP/RULE_ACL_IPV6/RULE_ACL_MAC match IPv4/IPv6/MAC
ACL rules. RULE_NET_SERVICE matches named net-services.
RULE_APPLICATION/RULE_APP_CATEGORY/RULE_WEB_CATEGORY match
application-aware traffic. The rule-type must be consistent
with the services configured under the condition.

type.description:
Condition type for the rule. Determines the base match
criteria category.

ArubaPolicy_PoliciesSchemaGET.description:
Policies define ordered sets of rules that match network
traffic and apply permit, deny, or advanced actions such as
NAT, redirect, and policy-based routing.

policy.description:
A firewall policy identified by a unique name. Each policy
contains an ordered set of rules that match traffic and
apply actions. ACL-type rules (RULE_ACL_IP, RULE_ACL_IPV6,
RULE_ACL_MAC) are only permitted in POLICY_NETWORK_ACL
policies.

association.description:
Specifies how this policy is associated with clients or
interfaces. Defaults to ASSOCIATION_ROLE (role-based
policy assignment).

description.description:
A user-defined description or comment for this policy.
Maximum 256 characters.

dsf-enable.description:
Enable distributed services firewall (DSF) for this policy.
When enabled, the policy type must be POLICY_TYPE_SECURITY.
Applicable to AOS-CX switches only. Defaults to false.

name.description:
Unique name of the policy. This is a required key field.
Maximum 145 characters. On AOS-CX, space (' ') and
question mark ('?') characters are not supported in
policy names.

type.description:
Type of policy. Determines the rule structure and actions
available. If dsf-enable is true, the type must be
POLICY_TYPE_SECURITY.

ArubaPolicy_PolicingActionGroup.description:
Grouping for QoS traffic policing policy actions. Supports
single-rate and two-rate three-color marker (trTCM) policing
to rate-limit traffic matching policy rules.

policing.description:
QoS traffic policing configuration for this policy rule.
Enforces rate limits on matching traffic using committed
information rate (CIR) and optional peak information rate
(PIR) parameters with configurable exceed actions.

committed-burst-size.description:
Committed Burst Size (CBS) in bytes. Maximum burst of
traffic allowed at the committed information rate before
packets are considered to exceed the rate.

committed-info-rate.description:
Committed Information Rate (CIR) in Kbps. Defines the
guaranteed bandwidth for matching traffic. Must be less
than the peak-info-rate (PIR) when two-rate policing
is configured.

exceed-action.description:
Action to take when traffic exceeds the configured rate.
For single-rate policing, this applies when CIR is
exceeded. For two-rate policing (POLICING_TR_TCM), this
applies when PIR is exceeded.

exceed-cir-dscp.description:
DSCP value to remark on packets that exceed the CIR but
are within the PIR. Only applicable when policing type is
POLICING_TR_TCM.

ArubaPolicy_PolicyRulesGroup.description:
Grouping for policy rules. Contains the ordered list of rules
that define the match conditions and actions for a policy.

policy-rule.description:
Ordered list of policy rules. Each rule defines a match
condition and action pair. Rules are evaluated in ascending
order by position (low to high). The key 'position' uniquely
identifies each rule within the policy.

description.description:
A user-defined description or comment for this policy rule.
Maximum length is 256 characters.

devices-applicable.description:
Read-only field indicating whether the rule is supported by
all or a subset of target devices. Values are 'full' (all
devices support this rule) or 'partial' (only some devices
support it).

position.description:
Sequence position that determines the order in which rules
are evaluated. This is a required key field. Lower values
are evaluated first. The position must be unique for each
rule within the policy.

Grouping for policy rules. Contains the ordered list of rules

ArubaPolicy_RedirectConfig.description:
Grouping for policy redirect configuration. Specifies the
redirection destination when the action type is ACTION_REDIRECT.

redirect.description:
Redirect configuration. Specifies where matched traffic
should be redirected to, such as a tunnel, tunnel-group,
or ESI (External Service Interface) group.

destination.description:
Specifies the redirect destination type. REDIRECT_TUNNEL
redirects to a specific GRE tunnel. REDIRECT_TUNNEL_GROUP
redirects to a tunnel group for load balancing. REDIRECT_ESI
redirects to an External Service Interface group.

x-enumDescriptions.REDIRECT_SLB: Redirect to Service Load Balancing (SLB) Group.

esi-direction.description:
Direction of ESI redirect (forward or reverse). Only
applicable when destination is REDIRECT_ESI.

esi-group.description:
ESI (External Service Interface) group name for redirect.
Only applicable when destination is REDIRECT_ESI. References
a configured SLB group.

tunnel.description:
Tunnel ID for redirect. Only applicable when destination is
REDIRECT_TUNNEL. Valid range: 1 to 16777215.

tunnel-group.description:
Name of the tunnel group for redirect. Only applicable when
destination is REDIRECT_TUNNEL_GROUP. Maximum 20 characters.

ArubaPolicy_RouteConfig.description: Grouping for route action configuration with optional NAT.

route.description:
Route configuration with optional destination NAT and
source NAT for ACTION_ROUTE rules.

ArubaPolicy_RouteConfigGet.description: Grouping for route action configuration with optional NAT.

route.description:
Route configuration with optional destination NAT and
source NAT for ACTION_ROUTE rules.

ArubaPolicy_RoutingActionConfig.description:
Grouping for Policy Based Routing (PBR) action configuration.
Defines how matched traffic is routed via nexthop lists, tunnels,
tunnel groups, or IPsec maps.

routing-action.description:
PBR action configuration: dscp tag and routing options for route ACL rules
   - forward: use routing table to forward traffic
   - route:
       - nexthop-list: use nexthop-list to forward traffic
       - tunnel: forward traffic to GRE tunnel
       - tunnel-group: forward traffic to tunnel-group
       - ipsec-map: forward traffic to ipsec tunnel with configured ipsec-map
   - cluster: forward to cluster (only for APs)

destination.description:
PBR nexthop type. This is a required field. NEXTHOP_LIST
routes via a configured nexthop list. IPSEC_MAP routes via
an IPsec tunnel. TUNNEL routes via a GRE tunnel. TUNNEL_GROUP
routes via a tunnel group. FORWARD uses the routing table.

dscp.description:
DSCP tag value to apply to PBR routed traffic.
Valid range: 1-63.

ipsec-map.description:
Name of the IPsec map for PBR routing through an IPsec
tunnel. Only applicable when destination is IPSEC_MAP.
Maximum 256 characters.

next-hop-list.description:
Name of the nexthop list for PBR routing. Only applicable
when destination is NEXTHOP_LIST. References a configured
nexthop group.

tunnel.description:
GRE tunnel ID for PBR routing. Only applicable when
destination is TUNNEL. For gateway devices only.

tunnel-group.description:
Tunnel group name for PBR routing. Only applicable when
destination is TUNNEL_GROUP. For gateway devices only.

ArubaPolicy_SecurityPolicyGroup.description:
Grouping for security policy configuration within a policy.
Contains the rule list, policy-level metadata, and optional
features like CALEA and URL redirection.

security-policy.description:
Security policy configuration for this policy. Contains the
policy type, ordered list of rules, and optional features
such as CALEA lawful intercept and URL redirection.

calea.description:
Enable Communications Assistance for Law Enforcement Act
(CALEA) lawful intercept on traffic matching this policy.
When true, matching traffic is mirrored for lawful
intercept purposes.

description.description:
A user-defined description or comment for the security
policy. Maximum 256 characters.

redirect-blocked-http-url.description:
When true, HTTP requests that are denied by this policy
are redirected to the configured redirect URL instead of
being silently dropped.

redirect-url.description:
URL to redirect denied HTTP traffic to when
redirect-blocked-http-url is enabled. Maximum 256
characters.

type.description:

are allowed in the policy rules (e.g., IPv4, IPv6,
Ethernet). Affects the valid rule-types for child rules.

x-enumDescriptions.PROMISCUOUS:
Set port as private-vlan promiscuous port. A promiscuous port
will be a member of the primary VLAN. Promiscuous ports can send
traffic to all ports of the primary VLAN as well as to the ports of
associated isolated/community VLANs. Promiscuous ports are used to
communicate outside the PVLAN domain.
x-enumDescriptions.SECONDARY:
Configure port as private-vlan secondary port. A secondary port will
be a member of a secondary VLAN. Secondary ports which belong to a
community VLAN can send traffic to all ports of the same community
VLAN as well as to the promiscuous ports of its primary VLAN. A port
belonging to an isolated VLAN can only send traffic to promiscuous
ports of its primary VLAN.

Each policy name must be unique, and each position must be unique within the group.

get.description:
Configure role and its associated gpid
Role is a feature that allows administrators to assign network access to clients
The network admin can create configuration profiles(roles), associate
them to clients and configure various parameters of role that are supported.
Roles are auto assigned with default gpid at time of roles creation
Users can assign custom gpid to existing role
